mechanism_narrative: A persistent multi-month rise in FEDFUNDS (currently 3.5%, up from 1% in mid-2004) raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the tightening cycle continues.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 60d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during rate-hike episodes are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration or reversal in HOUST (housing starts, near cycle highs at ~2050k units) signals an impending contraction in residential investment and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days once starts decline meaningfully from peak.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% from its trailing 3-month peak
failure_condition: Following triggered HOUST declines of >5%, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicates a stagflationary policy squeeze where the Fed must tighten into rising prices, compressing valuation multiples more severely than either series alone would predict, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3+ consecutive months while CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with UNRATE already low (4.9%) and PAYEMS still accelerating, further labor market tightening raises wage-cost and inflation risk, prompting markets to anticipate additional Fed tightening and reversing the typically positive equity response to strong labor prints, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5% and PAYEMS 3-month average gain exceeds 150k
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During periods of low UNRATE and strong PAYEMS growth, forward 60d SPX returns remain positive on average, contradicting the regime-flip hypothesis, or effect direction is unstable across at least two distinct low-unemployment episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in INDPRO growth below its trailing 12-month average signals weakening industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in slower supply-side growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below its trailing 12-month average for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Triggered INDPRO deceleration episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects resilient consumer demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Correlation between RSAFS monthly growth and subsequent 5d SPX returns falls below 0.03 Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or sign of relationship flips persistently for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with decelerating PAYEMS growth jointly signals genuine labor market deterioration rather than noise in either series individually, foreshadowing weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month average gain falls below 100k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show no statistically significant negative skew in forward 60d returns versus baseline (p > 0.10), or interaction model fails to outperform univariate UNRATE-only model in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above the trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rate, the real policy rate turns restrictive, historically preceding equity market weakness as financial conditions tighten in real terms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rate
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle transitioning to restrictive real rates
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following real-rate-positive crossings are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05), or effect reverses sign across two or more separate crossing episodes.
